D3 Chemistry L4 Analyze Reference

Fluorescence vs Phosphorescence — Singlet vs Triplet

absorb a photon, drop to S₁, then either emit immediately (fluorescence, ns) or cross to T₁ first and emit after milliseconds-to-seconds (phosphorescence)

The Jablonski diagram lays it out: absorption populates S₁ (or higher); after fast vibrational relaxation the molecule sits at the v=0 level of S₁; from there it can either (a) emit a photon and drop to S₀ — fluorescence, ns timescale, allowed transition, small Stokes shift; or (b) intersystem-cross (ISC) to the triplet T₁ and emit slowly — phosphorescence, ms-to-min, forbidden by spin, larger Stokes shift.

Toggle the pathway. The Jablonski diagram highlights the active transition; the time-resolved decay curve shows the contrasting lifetimes; the emission swatch shows the Stokes-shifted colour.
